blocks.content: Past midnight, Shirone and his group made their way through the Vatican's secret passageways, descending into a dimly lit corridor where the lights had been extinguished.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: At the end of this hallway lies the Inquisition.
Shirone's goal was to uncover the true nature of the Inquisition, making a discreet approach essential.
According to Seina, the guards rotated every eight hours.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Regardless of their rank, they're clergy. It's better not to use mental magic.
After a tense wait, the clock struck 2 AM. Footsteps echoed in the darkness.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Ugh, I'm so sleepy,

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Standing guard in the dark is brutal.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Yeah, especially the night shift.
At the end of the corridor, the relief team was already waiting.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Anything to report?

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Nothing. No one ever comes here.
Most of the guards didn't even know the Inquisition's exact location.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now's the time.
As if on cue, Shirone's group moved in unison.
Guided by faint, firefly-like light, they advanced until a large iron door blocked their path.
The metallic scent in the air was overwhelming.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can we do this?
Despite Seina's concern, Shirone skillfully manipulated her Miracle Stream, thinning it into a delicate thread and threading it into the lock mechanism.
Sound!
Realizing the potential noise, Seina looked up in alarm, but the lock clicked open soundlessly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Vacuum magic.
As the iron door creaked open, a cacophony of voices echoed out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah!
Shirone quickly shut the door, controlling the sound waves with precision.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: God! Punish me!
A scream pierced the air, and Seina's face turned pale.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: This… this is the Inquisition?
Iron doors lined the corridor like a prison, and from behind them came horrifying sounds.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: No… this can't be.
Even for a basement, this was too far removed from the sanctity and dignity expected of the Vatican.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: I wouldn't have thought this way before receiving your blessing.
Seina couldn't argue.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I already knew.
Though she'd never seen it with her own eyes, she'd always had an inkling of how the Inquisition dealt with heretics.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: It wasn't fear of what she now realized. The true horror was…
How could I live so blindly, without questioning any of this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's go. This is just the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: The beginning?
Shirone's gaze pierced the darkness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Paladins are trained from childhood. Do you know where?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Of course not. We were too young, and by the time I could remember, all I knew was it was somewhere deep in the mountains.
Shirone pointed into the darkness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Beyond this place lies the paladin training grounds. The Vatican isn't just the church itself—north of here, the mountains stretch out, dotted with numerous villages. Those who are sentenced by the Inquisition are exiled there.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: And there… is where paladins are trained?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's a question we need to confirm for ourselves. Let's move.
As they advanced, Seina became lost in thought.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: The grueling training of my childhood.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: It had been a grueling ordeal, both physically and mentally.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: But I survived. I lived with pride, defeating countless evils.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: How had things come to this?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: I need to reclaim my divine power. As Yahweh said, I must confirm the truth and decide for myself.
As they passed the iron bars, Seina glimpsed the horrors inside.

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Kill me! Just kill me!

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: I was wrong! I was so foolish! Please forgive me, O Lord!

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Hehehe! Yes, more! Let me taste heaven!
Seina's hair stood on end.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: This… this is just like the paladins of the Inquisition.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When thinking itself becomes a torment, the human mind simplifies to its most primal state. That's why they're so fanatical—and strong.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Seina had once been strong, too.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: But that's not true strength. Real strength lies not in destruction, but in the power to protect.
At that moment, an iron door creaked open, and an Inquisitor emerged, his body drenched in blood and tears of blood streaking his face.

blocks.speaker: Inquisitor
blocks.content: I… I am so sad. This world… this reality…
Despite Shirone's group standing nearby, he walked past them like a machine, heading toward another room.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's completely gone. He doesn't even see us. His mind is paralyzed, and he's operating on autopilot.
Inside one of the opened rooms, a brutally beaten man lay collapsed.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: He's lost too much blood. Hold on, I'll stop the bleeding—

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Kill me,

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Please… just kill me and free me from this prison.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: I've seen enough!

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Yahweh was right. I was wrong. There's no need to investigate further. Let's leave and rescue these people first.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That would have been the logical decision, but Shirone remained deep in thought.

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Kill me! I said kill me!
The man weakly flailed his arms, his strength drained.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: It's okay now. We'll get you out of here.

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Kehehe.
A scraping sound came from the man's throat.

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Kehehehe! Get me out? Of what?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: From this prison, of course—
Suddenly, the man's eyes focused sharply for the first time.

blocks.speaker: Prisoner
blocks.content: Is there a prison this vast?
As everyone grappled with the meaning of his words, the man lunged forward, screaming.
Blood splattered across Seina's face as she recoiled in terror, just as she stumbled backward.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: It's pointless.
An eerie voice came from beyond the iron gate.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: When did he get here?
A man with a robe that trailed to the floor stood holding chains in both hands.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Vanisa.
He was second in rank in the Holy See, known as the Forgiver.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What is going on here?

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: …God.
When Vanisa removed his hood, the robe slipped off his body like a snake shedding its skin.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: What has he done to his body?
Certain parts of his flesh were missing, exposing bones, and his abdomen bore twisted marks, as if his organs had been contorted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll handle this. He's strong.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Kill… Kill him.
The tortured man groaned in agony as Vanisa pointed with his scythe.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Taking him out of that prison is mercy.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Prison?

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: We are all imprisoned. Right here.
He thrust his scythe into his own chest.
Boom!
Before the echo of the explosion subsided, Shirone shouted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Go! Find the exit!
Understanding Vanisa's strength, Neid and Iruki grabbed the weakened Seina by the wrist and ran down the corridor.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Conviction, flesh, philosophy, movement.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Absolutely not, Yahweh. It's an illusion.
Snapping a protruding rib, he seized his scythe and charged again.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: There is no way to escape God's prison.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Maya.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: He was stationed in the Delta Headquarters in the Tormia district, guarding the artists' residence, where he had been assigned the room next to Maya's.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Guarding Maya is enough. I don't need anything more.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: That's when he felt something strange in his lower half.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: What?
Looking down, he found his pants undone, and his right hand moving on its own.
Ahhh! What the hell! Damn it!

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: God's Hand Syndrome.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: You stupid hand! Stop it! I'm not some monkey!
As his left hand wrestled his right, Maya burst through the door.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Kaiden! What's going on?

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Ahhh! Ahhhhhh!
Kaiden was suddenly grateful for his body's superhuman reflexes.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: S-Sorry! I didn't see anything! I swear!

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Damn it! Damn it!

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: I'm hopeless. I'll never be able to protect Maya.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: As if to contradict his thoughts, his right hand raised its index finger and slowly moved it side to side.
